novel_features.name: Appeal-chain walker
novel_features.description: Walk the cassation, conclusie, and eerdere-aanleg graph for a Dutch court decision in both directions and print the full chain as a tree or JSON.
novel_features.rationale: Requires recursively fetching content, parsing the psi: relations, and walking the appeal graph — no existing CLI does this, and the API surfaces the relations only one hop at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this whenever an agent needs the full procedural history of a Dutch decision (cassation chain, A-G conclusie, lower-court rulings) in one call instead of clicking through rechtspraak.nl tabs by hand.

novel_features.name: Vindplaatsen extractor
novel_features.description: Extract the citation list (vindplaatsen) for a decision and emit as BibTeX, CSV, or JSON ready for legal-brief footnotes.
novel_features.rationale: rechtspraak-js flags vindplaatsen as a data-quality problem; no tool surfaces them as structured first-class output, even though every legal brief needs them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent is drafting a Dutch legal brief and needs the citation list for a cited decision without parsing raw RDF/XML.

novel_features.name: Dossier by zaaknummer
novel_features.description: List every decision sharing a case number across instances, sorted chronologically.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local index on zaaknummer that the upstream search endpoint does not expose as a filter; only possible after sync.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when tracking a single Dutch case across the rechtbank, gerechtshof, and Hoge Raad — the API offers no case-file filter, only the local index does.

novel_features.name: Watch / incremental new-decision poll
novel_features.description: Poll for new decisions matching a filter, dedupe against the local SQLite, and emit only the new ECLIs — cron-friendly with --quiet.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local persistence to dedupe; without the local store every poll repeats the same Atom feed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Wire this into a cron or agent loop to receive only newly-published Dutch court decisions on your filter — no manual rechtspraak.nl refreshing.

novel_features.name: Conclusie pairing
novel_features.description: Given a Hoge Raad decision ECLI, return the matching A-G conclusie (or reverse).
novel_features.rationale: Walks the psi:conclusie relation as a one-shot; the API exposes the relation but no tool turns it into a paired lookup.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent reads a Hoge Raad ruling, immediately pull the Advocate-General's conclusie so the reasoning context is in scope.

novel_features.name: Bulk archive ingest
novel_features.description: Download the official weekly full-corpus archive and bulk-insert to the local SQLite — orders of magnitude faster than paging the Atom feed for backfills.
novel_features.rationale: The Atom feed is fine for tail updates but useless for multi-year backfills; the archive ZIP is the right primitive for corpus work and no CLI uses it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when bootstrapping a research corpus or rebuilding the local DB; live sync against the Atom feed is the wrong tool for backfills.

novel_features.name: Court-code dictionary
novel_features.description: Bidirectional offline lookup against the Instanties controlled vocabulary — court code to full name and PSI URI, or fuzzy name to code.
novel_features.rationale: Court PSI URIs are unfriendly (http://standaarden.overheid.nl/owms/terms/Hoge_Raad_der_Nederlanden); a local dictionary command eliminates that friction for every other command.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to resolve a Dutch court name, code, or PSI URI without round-tripping to the API, this is the lookup.

novel_features.name: MCP server mode
novel_features.description: Expose every rechtspraak command as an MCP tool — the first MCP server for Dutch case law.
novel_features.rationale: No MCP server exists for rechtspraak today. Every absorbed and novel command becomes an MCP tool automatically.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Wire this into Claude Desktop or any MCP-aware agent to give it native access to Dutch court decisions, citations, and case-files.

novel_features.name: ECLI parse/validate
novel_features.description: Pure-local regex parse of an ECLI into country, court, year, and sequence — with validation — and `ecli url` for the deeplink.
novel_features.rationale: Every other command needs a parsed ECLI; exposing the parser as a command makes it agent-composable without a network round-trip.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this whenever an agent needs to validate or destructure an ECLI before fetching content or chaining into another command.

novel_features.name: Narrowing pipe
novel_features.description: Read an ECLI list from stdin (or piped from search/watch/sync), fetch each decision's content from the live API (paced via the shared rate limiter), apply local include/exclude/phrase/regex filters against title+summary+body, and emit the narrowed list — the defining workflow for keyword-overlap-heavy Dutch legal research. Bounded by --max-pages on upstream input scope; one HTTP call per ECLI.
novel_features.rationale: The upstream API has no free-text search; long-tail Dutch legal terms produce 1000+ results per topic. Iterative local narrowing over the synced corpus is the only way to cut a result set without throwing away precision.
novel_features.example: rechtspraak-pp-cli search --subject strafrecht --court HR --from 2024-01-01 --agent | rechtspraak-pp-cli narrow --keyword huurprijs --exclude "kort geding" --phrase "huurprijswijziging" --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a metadata-only search returns hundreds or thousands of decisions and an agent needs to narrow by phrasing — chain it after search, watch, or sync. The same vocabulary (`--keyword`, `--exclude`, `--phrase`, `--regex`) also lives on `search` directly for single-shot use.
novel_features.group: Search narrowing for long-tail legal terms

narrative.headline: The first CLI for Dutch court decisions with rich local narrowing for long-tail legal keywords, an appeal-chain walker, and an MCP server.
narrative.value_prop: Every Dutch ECLI from data.rechtspraak.nl, indexed locally so include/exclude/phrase/regex narrowing cuts a 1000-result topic down to a usable set — the upstream API has no free-text search, so this is the only place that workflow exists. Walks the cassation chain in one command, exports vindplaatsen as BibTeX, and exposes every command as an MCP tool — the first MCP server for Dutch case law.

quickstart.comment: Pull the last week of decisions into the local SQLite cache.
List recent Hoge Raad tax-law decisions — agent-shaped output.
Pull the inhoudsindicatie (summary) for a specific decision.
Walk the full appeal chain (rechtbank → hof → Hoge Raad plus A-G conclusie).
Export the vindplaatsen as BibTeX for a brief's footnotes.
Run as an MCP server for Claude Desktop or any MCP-aware agent.

troubleshoots.symptom: search with `--keyword` or `--phrase` returns zero results despite expected matches
troubleshoots.fix: Keyword/phrase filtering is local-only and requires the corpus to be synced first. Run `rechtspraak-pp-cli sync --court <code> --from <date>` to populate the FTS index, then re-run the search.

troubleshoots.symptom: search returns more results than expected even with `--procedure cassatie`
troubleshoots.fix: The upstream API silently ignores `procedure=`; the CLI filters locally against Proceduresoorten metadata after sync. Confirm the procedure name resolves with `rechtspraak-pp-cli procedures list`.

troubleshoots.symptom: search returns empty results from the API
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm the subject area name resolves to a PSI URI with `rechtspraak-pp-cli subjects list` — many spelling variants exist.

troubleshoots.symptom: get ECLI returns metadata only, no summary
troubleshoots.fix: Pass `--summary` or `--full` to request `return=DOC` from the content endpoint.

troubleshoots.symptom: Atom feed paging is slow for multi-year backfills
troubleshoots.fix: Use `sync archive --week <yyyy-Www>` instead — the official weekly archive is orders of magnitude faster.
Run `rechtspraak-pp-cli code <name-or-code>` to see the canonical name and PSI URI.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent needs to research, monitor, or correlate Dutch court decisions. Strong fit for Dutch legal research, appellate workflow assistance, jurisprudence-corpus building, and any agent that needs structured access to ECLIs without parsing raw RDF/XML. Pairs naturally with `tenderned` and `pdok-location` for a full Dutch civic-data agent stack.

recipes.title: Iteratively narrow an over-broad result set
recipes.command: rechtspraak-pp-cli uitspraken search --subject strafrecht --court HR --from 2024-01-01 --keyword huurprijs --exclude "kort geding" --phrase "huurprijswijziging" --annotate-count --agent
recipes.explanation: Upstream API returns metadata-only matches; the --keyword/--exclude/--phrase flags filter the result set locally against the FTS5 index — --annotate-count prints how many decisions survive after each filter pass.

recipes.title: Walk the full procedural history of a Hoge Raad decision
recipes.explanation: The response is deeply nested across instances; the dotted --select projection keeps the agent's context lean by emitting only the chain skeleton.

recipes.title: Daily new-decision watch with narrowing
recipes.explanation: Exits silently if no new decisions match both the upstream filter (HR + belastingrecht + last 24h) AND the local keyword filter — drop-in for cron + mail.

recipes.title: Pipe-compose narrowing across commands
recipes.command: rechtspraak-pp-cli uitspraken search --court HR --from 2024-01-01 --quiet | rechtspraak-pp-cli narrow --keyword belastingrecht --exclude conclusie --agent
recipes.explanation: Read ECLIs from any source, apply local filters, emit narrowed list — the same vocabulary as search but composable across pipelines.

recipes.title: Bibliography export for a brief
recipes.explanation: Emits the vindplaatsen as BibTeX entries with stable keys — paste directly into a LaTeX brief.

recipes.title: Resolve a court name to its PSI URI
recipes.explanation: Fully offline after first sync; useful in agent chains that need to construct a search filter.

trigger_phrases: narrow Dutch case search by keyword
